page: Google Cloud Platform t2d-standard-60 is a general-purpose server instance belonging to the t2d family. It features 60 vCPUs mapped to 60 physical cores of an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor, operating on the x86_64 architecture with a dedicated CPU allocation and 1.0 thread per core. The system is equipped with 240.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced memory-to-core ratio of 4.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or bundled GPU accelerators.

In benchmark evaluations, the instance demonstrates strong multi-core capabilities. The stress-ng multi-core CPU performance and bzip3 multi-threaded decompression both rank in the top tier, placing in the top 10% of tested servers. Single-core performance is also competitive, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance and PassMark CPU scores placing in the top 25%. Memory performance is mixed; while cached read-only small block memory bandwidth is strong, uncached large block read and write bandwidths fall into the average category.

The instance is well-suited for multi-threaded workloads such as software compilation, database operations, and image processing, which all scored in the top 25% in Geekbench and PassMark tests. However, it exhibits limitations in large-scale machine learning tasks; while prompt processing for small and medium LLM models is strong, text generation for large 70B models is weak, placing in the bottom 25%. This makes the server highly effective for general-purpose multi-threaded applications, web serving, and database management, but less optimal for heavy LLM text generation.
description: The Google Cloud Platform t2d-standard-60 is an x86_64 general-purpose server powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor. It features 60 dedicated physical cores with 1.0 thread per core and 240.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators. In benchmark testing, the server delivers top-tier multi-core CPU performance and multi-threaded decompression, both placing in the top 10%. Single-core processing, software compilation, and database operations also rank strongly in the top 25%. However, uncached memory bandwidth remains average, and large-model LLM text generation is a notable performance bottleneck. This hardware profile is highly effective for multi-threaded workloads, database operations, and software compilation, offering a balanced resource density without the added cost of specialized accelerator hardware.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform t2d-standard-60 is a general-purpose server with 60 dedicated AMD EPYC cores and 240 GB RAM, delivering top-tier multi-core performance for databases and compilation.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform t2d-standard-60 features 60 dedicated AMD EPYC cores and 240 GB RAM, delivering top-tier multi-core performance.
tagline: A dedicated 60-core AMD EPYC processor with balanced memory, delivering top-tier multi-threaded performance for databases and software compilation.
bullet_points: 60 dedicated physical AMD EPYC 7B13 cores with 1.0 thread per core.
240.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core.
Top-tier multi-core CPU and multi-threaded decompression performance.
Strong performance in database operations, software compilation, and image processing.
No local storage or GPU accelerators included in the configuration.
Weak performance in large-model LLM text generation tasks.
